title: Exit Readiness
ask_prompt: My exit readiness score is {pct}/100 (Grade {grade}). Key weaknesses: {weaknesses}. Revenue: {revenue}, Net margin: {netMargin}%, Growth: {growth}. What should I prioritize to become exit-ready in 12 months?
headline_a: Your business metrics are strong. You could approach buyers or brokers with confidence.
headline_b: Most fundamentals are solid. A few targeted improvements will make you exit-ready.
headline_c: Potential is there, but several areas need strengthening before an exit process.
headline_d: Significant work needed across multiple dimensions before the business is attractive to buyers.
factor_profitability_desc: Consistent positive net margins attract higher multiples
factor_growth_desc: Consistent growth trajectory increases buyer confidence
factor_margins_desc: Higher margins signal pricing power and scalability
factor_predictability_desc: Low variance in monthly revenue reduces buyer risk
factor_runway_desc: Strong cash runway = no desperation sale pressure
factor_data_quality_desc: Clean financials speed up due diligence
action_profitability: Cut underperforming products, renegotiate supplier terms, raise prices on high-demand items
action_growth: Diversify channels, invest in marketing, launch new products, enter new markets
action_margins: Negotiate better supplier pricing, reduce waste, move to higher-margin product mix
action_predictability: Build subscription/recurring revenue, secure long-term contracts, smooth seasonal gaps
action_runway: Build cash reserves, collect receivables faster, reduce discretionary spending
action_data_quality: Connect all sales channels, maintain consistent records, digitize receipts
action_diversification: Add online/offline channels, expand product categories, reduce customer concentration
